contents.value: **@interface** — Objective-C keyword.
**@implementation** — Objective-C keyword.
**@end** — Objective-C keyword.
**@protocol** — Objective-C keyword.
**@property** — Objective-C keyword.
**NSLog** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**alloc** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**init** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**dealloc** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**copy** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.

A **pointer** stores the memory address of another variable.
```c
int *ptr = &value;
```
A **struct** groups related data fields into a single composite type.
An **enum** defines a type with a fixed set of named constants.
A **union** stores different data types in the same memory location.

**typedef** creates an alias for an existing type.
```c
typedef unsigned long ulong;
```

**malloc** allocates a block of memory on the heap and returns a pointer.
```c
int *p = malloc(n * sizeof(int));
```

**free** releases previously allocated heap memory.
```c
free(ptr);
```
**sizeof** returns the size in bytes of a type or variable.
**volatile** tells the compiler a variable may change unexpectedly (e.g., hardware register).
**static** limits scope to the current file/function, or preserves value between calls.
**extern** declares a variable or function defined in another translation unit.
**inline** suggests the compiler insert the function body at the call site.
**const** marks a value as read-only after initialization.
**register** hints the compiler to store the variable in a CPU register.

**Preprocessor directives** run before compilation.
```c
#include, #define, #ifdef, #pragma
```

```objective-c
@interface NSString : NSObject
```
The fundamental immutable string class in Foundation framework, representing Unicode text with rich manipulation capabilities. NSString objects are created using @"literal" syntax or factory methods.

```objective-c
@interface NSArray<ObjectType> : NSObject
```
An ordered immutable collection of objects in Foundation framework, created using @[] literal syntax. Elements are accessed by zero-based index and must be Objective-C objects, not primitive types.

```objective-c
@interface NSDictionary<KeyType, ObjectType> : NSObject
```
An immutable collection of key-value pairs in Foundation framework, created using @{} literal syntax. Keys must conform to NSCopying protocol and are typically NSString objects.

```objective-c
@interface NSNumber : NSValue
```
An immutable object wrapper for C numeric types including int, float, double, and BOOL. Created using @() literal syntax or factory methods like numberWithInt: for boxing primitives.

```objective-c
@interface NSMutableArray<ObjectType> : NSArray
```
A mutable ordered collection that extends NSArray with methods for adding, removing, and replacing elements. Created using [NSMutableArray array] or [[NSMutableArray alloc] init] factory patterns.

```objective-c
@interface NSMutableDictionary<KeyType, ObjectType> : NSDictionary
```
A mutable collection of key-value pairs extending NSDictionary with methods for adding, updating, and removing entries. Commonly used for building dynamic configuration and state containers.

```objective-c
void dispatch_async(dispatch_queue_t queue, dispatch_block_t block)
```
Submits a block for asynchronous execution on the specified Grand Central Dispatch queue and returns immediately. Essential for offloading work to background threads and updating UI on the main queue.

```objective-c
dispatch_queue_t dispatch_queue_create(const char *label, dispatch_queue_attr_t attr)
```
Creates a new dispatch queue with the specified label and attributes. Pass DISPATCH_QUEUE_SERIAL for serial execution or DISPATCH_QUEUE_CONCURRENT for concurrent task processing.

```objective-c
@selector(methodName:)
```
Compiler directive that returns a SEL type representing the specified method selector, used for target-action patterns, performSelector calls, and dynamic method invocation in Objective-C runtime.

```objective-c
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```objective-c
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```objective-c
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```objective-c
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```objective-c
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```objective-c
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```objective-c
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```objective-c
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```objective-c
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
